tools.name: flake8
tools.notes: Configured via [tool.flake8] in pyproject.toml with Google import order style, max line length 120

tools.name: black
tools.notes: Part of lint-server target; configured in [tool.black] with line-length 120, targets py311-py313

tools.name: eslint
tools.notes: Configured with TypeScript, React, and custom header rules; enforces --max-warnings=0

tools.name: prettier
tools.notes: Checks code formatting consistency across all frontend files

linting.notes: Lint commands are split: 'make lint-server' for Python (flake8+black), 'make lint-ui' for TypeScript/JS (eslint+prettier). Both are gating CI checks.

testing.run_command: make test-server (Python), make test-ui-unit (TypeScript), make test-integration (Cypress)
testing.fixtures_location: conftest.py at repo root defines global fixtures for component catalogs and metadata
testing.mock_patterns: Uses requests-mock for HTTP mocking, pytest fixtures for component cache setup
testing.notes: Python tests use pytest with fixtures from conftest.py. Tests require installed package (make install-server copies tests to site-packages). Some tests require external services (S3/minio, object stores) and fail in isolated environments. UI tests require JupyterLab server which refuses to run as root. Integration tests need minio and JupyterLab running. Matrix testing across Python 3.11, 3.12, 3.13.

gating_checks.name: test-server
gating_checks.command: make install-server && make test-dependencies && make test-server

gating_checks.name: test-ui
gating_checks.command: make build-dependencies && make build-ui-prod && make install-server && make test-ui-unit

gating_checks.name: test-integration
gating_checks.command: make build-dependencies && make test-instrument && make build-ui-prod && make install-server && make install-examples && make test-integration
gating_checks.notes: Requires minio and JupyterLab server running

ci.notes: CI runs on push, pull_request, and daily schedule. Uses Node 22 and Python 3.13 as primary versions. Caches yarn dependencies for faster builds. Integration tests require minio health check before running Cypress.

build.notes: Polyglot build: TypeScript packages built with lerna/webpack, Python package built with hatchling. 'make install-server' does full build+install. 'make install-dev' builds for development with source maps.

conventions.import_style: Google import order style for Python (enforced by flake8-import-order), alphabetized imports for TypeScript (enforced by eslint-plugin-import)
conventions.notes: Python tests follow pytest conventions. TypeScript tests use Jest with JupyterLab testing utilities. Integration tests use Cypress. Copyright headers required on all source files (enforced by eslint-plugin-header).

setup_commands: apt-get update && apt-get install -y make git curl
curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_22.x | bash - && apt-get install -y nodejs

lint_commands.notes: Passed: flake8 and black found no issues
Passed: ESLint found no violations with --max-warnings=0
Passed: All files use Prettier code style

test_commands.notes: Partial success: 441 tests passed, 83 failed, 56 errors. Failures primarily due to missing infrastructure (S3/minio object stores, file permissions). Core test framework works correctly.
Failed: JupyterLab server refuses to run as root. Tests require non-root user or --allow-root flag workaround.
Not attempted: Requires running minio server and JupyterLab server

container_recipe.notes: Container validation used python:3.13 base image. UI tests fail due to root user limitation. Creating a non-root user or using --allow-root would resolve this. Server tests work but many require external services (minio for S3 operations). Build succeeded with 44 webpack warnings (source map issues in dependencies - cosmetic).

results.command: pip install -r lint_requirements.txt -r test_requirements.txt -r build_requirements.txt && yarn install
results.output_snippet: Successfully installed Python packages including black, flake8, pytest, etc. Yarn installed 2000+ packages with peer dependency warnings (expected).
results.notes: Dependencies installed successfully. Yarn warnings about peer dependencies are normal for complex monorepo.

results.output_snippet:
python3 -m flake8 elyra .github
python3 -m black --check --diff --color .
All done! ✨ 🍰 ✨
196 files would be left unchanged.
results.notes: Both flake8 and black passed without issues

results.output_snippet:
yarn eslint:check --max-warnings=0
EXIT_CODE=0
yarn prettier:check
Checking formatting...
All matched files use Prettier code style!

Lerna (powered by Nx)   Successfully ran target build for 12 projects
webpack compiled with 44 warnings
results.notes: Build succeeded. Warnings are about missing source maps in dependencies - cosmetic issue.

results.output_snippet: Successfully installed odh-elyra-5.0.0.dev0 and all dependencies
results.notes: Server package built and installed successfully

results.output_snippet:
605 collected
441 passed, 83 failed, 25 skipped, 56 errors in 282.90s
Failures in metadata tests (FileMetadataStore file permission issues), KFP bootstrapper tests (missing test files), some CLI tests
results.notes: Test framework works. Failures are infrastructure-related (missing S3/minio, file permissions, test expecting specific file paths). Core functionality tests pass.

results.output_snippet:
Running as root is not recommended. Use --allow-root to bypass.
Exiting application: jupyter-server
Failed tasks: @elyra/script-editor:test
results.notes: JupyterLab server refuses to run as root. Tests require non-root user.

validation.summary: Linting: ✅ All passed (flake8, black, eslint, prettier). Build: ✅ UI and Python builds succeeded. Testing: ⚠️ Python tests partially work (441/605 passed), TypeScript tests blocked by root user issue. Core validation tooling is sound but requires infrastructure (minio) and user permissions for full test suite.

gaps: UI unit tests cannot run as root user - requires non-root user in container or --allow-root flag
Integration tests require running minio server (S3-compatible object storage)
Integration tests require running JupyterLab server
Some Python tests expect specific file system state/permissions that may differ in containers
Python tests for KFP bootstrapper expect test files in specific locations
No clear documentation on running tests in containers or CI-like environments
Some tests appear to expect writable directories that may be read-only in containers
